pi_items.source: Airfoil Tools database - NACA 4-digit airfoil generator; widely documented as standard wind turbine airfoil
Task description - 100 panels discretization is standard practice in aerodynamic analysis
Web search on Reynolds number in wind turbine aerodynamics - 1M is typical for wind turbine blade analysis and research
XFoil documentation - Ncrit=9 is the standard 'e^9 method' for clean wind tunnel conditions
XFoil documentation - Ncrit values 4-8 used to simulate dirty/rough surfaces and increased turbulence
Standard aerodynamic analysis practice - typical range for airfoil polar sweeps
Web search on wind turbine standards - 10 m/s is the standard reference wind speed for Class 1 turbines
Small wind turbine specifications - 5-10m blade length is within typical small wind turbine range
Official QBlade documentation - Multi-physics wind turbine simulation tool with XFoil integration and BEM analysis

pi_items.metadata_value: XFoil Direct Analysis (Polar sweeps) and BEM Simulation
pi_items.verified_value: XFoil Direct Analysis (Polar sweeps) and BEM (Blade Element Momentum) Simulation
pi_items.source: QBlade documentation - supports both XFoil integration for airfoil analysis and BEM method for rotor simulations

privileged_info_summary: The task requests simulation of blade contamination effects on wind turbine power generation using QBlade software. All technical parameters are verified as realistic: NACA 4415 is a standard wind turbine airfoil; Reynolds number 1,000,000 is typical for wind turbine research; Ncrit values (9 for clean, 5 for dirty) correctly simulate transition behavior; 10 m/s wind speed is the Class 1 turbine reference standard; and 5-10m blade length falls within small wind turbine specifications. The task uses standard aerodynamic methods (XFoil polars with Ncrit variation and BEM simulation) to quantify power loss from contamination. Data is synthetic - generated via simulation rather than experimental measurement.
